summary: A WhatsApp-like full-featured messaging app named 'Xeta' with real-time chat, user accounts, contacts, media sharing, and other core WhatsApp-style features. Includes WhatsApp-style bottom navigation (Chats, Updates, Calls, Settings), searchable/persistent user storage, larger logo in the top bar, footer copyright removed, unique username enforcement, and sticky header/footer on the main Chats tab only. Xeta AI feature has been permanently removed.
architectural_notes: Replacing Bluetooth-based architecture with internet-based real-time messaging using WebSockets or similar technology
Requires backend user authentication, message storage, and real-time event broadcasting
Username uniqueness must be enforced at both the backend (createUser in main.mo) and frontend (registration screen) with clear error messages
known_issues: After sign-in, the next screen fails to load / takes too long, and the keyboard flickers (shows and hides repeatedly) during loading
Registration fails on the registration screen and the app does not navigate to the next screen after submitting registration
Registration screen shows 'connection not ready, please try again' error when submitting name and username

existing_features.title: Implement a Bluetooth device discovery and pairing flow on the frontend using the Web Bluetooth API (navigator.bluetooth.requestDevice). Allow the user to scan for nearby Bluetooth devices, select one to pair with, and establish a GATT connection for data transfer.
Display a real-time connection status indicator in the chat UI that shows whether a Bluetooth device is currently connected, the name of the paired device, and a disconnect button.

Build a chat interface showing the full conversation between the two connected devices. Messages sent by the local user appear on the right; messages received from the paired Bluetooth device appear on the left. Each message bubble must display sender label and timestamp. Include a text input field and a send button.

Apply a clean, modern dark-mode visual theme with a deep navy/charcoal background, electric cyan accent colors, rounded message bubbles, and a monospace-friendly sans-serif font. The design should feel technical and wireless-communication-inspired. Enforce this style consistently across all screens.

Add a visible, styled scrollbar to the first page (BluetoothPairing view) so that all content is accessible when the viewport height is insufficient. The page container should allow vertical scrolling with overflow-y set appropriately, ensuring bottom elements such as buttons and status indicators are reachable by scrolling.
Remove the footer copyright line from the app. The footer element (or the copyright text within it) rendered in App.tsx should be deleted so it no longer appears on any screen.
Update the app header/top bar in App.tsx to display 'XetaChat' as the app name, replacing any previous name or placeholder text currently shown there.

Save the user-uploaded logo image as a static asset at 'frontend/public/assets/generated/xetachat-logo.png' and display it in the app header/top bar in App.tsx, replacing the current signal bar decoration or any existing logo placeholder. The logo should be sized appropriately for a top bar (approximately 32–40px in height), maintain its aspect ratio, and be vertically centered alongside the 'XetaChat' app name text.

In App.tsx, remove any 'Bluetooth Messenger' subtitle or secondary text currently displayed in the top bar/header alongside the XetaChat logo and app name, so that only the logo and the 'XetaChat' text remain in the header.

In App.tsx, resize the 'XetaChat' app name text in the header so that its font size visually matches the height of the XetaChat logo image (which is approximately 32–40px tall). The text should appear the same height as the logo and remain vertically centered alongside it.

Implement a user registration and login flow. On first launch, display a screen where the user enters a username and display name to create their account. Store the identity tied to Internet Identity. After registration, direct the user to the main chat screen. If already registered, skip to the main screen automatically.

Build a contacts screen that lists all registered users. Each contact entry shows the user's avatar (or initials placeholder), display name, username, and online/last seen status. Tapping a contact opens a one-on-one chat with that user.

Build a one-on-one chat screen for messaging between two users. Messages from the current user appear on the right in a colored bubble; received messages appear on the left in a contrasting bubble. Each bubble shows the message text and a timestamp. A text input field and send button are present at the bottom. Sending a message calls sendMessage on the backend. The message list auto-scrolls to the latest message.

Add message delivery and read receipt indicators to each message bubble in the chat screen. Show a single tick (sent), double tick (delivered), and blue/colored double tick (read) icon next to each sent message, reflecting the message's status field from the backend.

Implement group chat creation and messaging. Add a 'New Group' button on the contacts/chats screen. Group creation flow: select participants from contacts, set a group name. Group chats appear in the chat list alongside one-on-one chats. Inside a group chat, all messages from all members are displayed with sender names shown above each received message bubble.

Add a user profile screen accessible from the main navigation. Display the user's avatar (with ability to update), display name, username, and status message. Allow editing display name and status message, which calls updateProfile on the backend. Show an 'Online' presence indicator.

Display online/last seen presence indicators throughout the app. In the contacts list, contact header in chat, and group member views, show 'Online' if the user was recently active or 'Last seen [relative time]' otherwise. Update lastSeen on the backend whenever the current user sends a message or opens the app.

Implement media sharing in chat: allow users to attach and send images and files by tapping a paperclip/attachment icon in the chat input area. Uploaded media should be stored as base64-encoded content or URL references in the message payload on the backend. Received image messages render as inline image previews in the chat bubble; file messages render as a downloadable file link.

Redesign the entire app visual theme to match a modern WhatsApp-inspired aesthetic for 'Xeta'. Use a dark green and deep charcoal color palette (replacing the existing navy/cyan Bluetooth theme). Apply a clean sans-serif font, subtle card-style chat list rows, a distinct teal/green accent for send buttons and highlights, white/light message bubbles for received and accent-colored bubbles for sent messages. Rename all user-visible branding from 'XetaChat' / 'BlueChat' to 'Xeta'. Update index.html title to 'Xeta'. Apply the theme consistently across all screens: registration, contacts, chat, profile.

Build a main chat list screen (home screen after login) that shows all active one-on-one and group conversations. Each row displays the contact/group avatar or initials, name, last message preview text, and timestamp of the last message. Conversations are sorted by most recent activity. Tapping a row opens the corresponding chat.

Fix the post-sign-in navigation flow in App.tsx so that after a successful Internet Identity login, the app immediately transitions to the appropriate screen (registration if new user, chat-list if already registered) without indefinite loading. Add a clear loading state with a timeout/fallback so the user is never stuck on a blank or perpetual loading screen.

Eliminate the keyboard/virtual-keyboard flickering (rapid show/hide cycles) that occurs during the loading phase after sign-in in App.tsx and RegistrationScreen.tsx. Ensure that no input element receives and loses focus repeatedly during loading transitions, and that auto-focus on input fields is only triggered once the target screen has fully mounted and the loading state is resolved.

Fix the registration flow in RegistrationScreen.tsx so that calling createUser on the backend succeeds without errors. Add proper error handling that surfaces a readable error message to the user if registration fails, and ensure the component correctly awaits the mutation result before attempting navigation.

Fix the post-registration navigation in App.tsx so that after a successful createUser call, the app transitions from the registration screen to the chat list screen immediately. Ensure the currentUser / profile state is updated before the navigation check runs, so the app does not loop back to registration or get stuck on a loading screen.

Add a dedicated 'Xeta AI' entry pinned at the very top of the chat list screen, above the 'New Chat' button. It should appear as a special bot contact row with an avatar/icon labeled 'Xeta AI'. Tapping it opens a chat thread where the user can send messages and receive simulated AI responses (rule-based or canned responses). This entry must always be visible and never filtered out by search.

Replace the current app footer/bottom area with a WhatsApp-style bottom navigation bar containing four tabs: Chats (shows the main chat list), Updates (shows a placeholder status/stories section), Calls (shows a placeholder call history section), and Settings (shows the app settings and profile screen). Each tab must have a recognizable icon and label. The active tab must be visually highlighted. The bottom nav must be present on all main screens after login.

Add a search/filter bar at the top of the chat list screen that allows users to search for any registered user by display name or username. The search should query all users stored in the backend. Matching users not yet in a conversation should appear as results that can be tapped to start a new chat.

Increase the size of the Xeta logo image displayed in the app top bar so it is larger and more prominent. The logo height should be increased to approximately 48 to 56px while maintaining its aspect ratio and remaining vertically centered alongside the 'Xeta' app name text.
Remove any remaining footer copyright section or copyright text from the app. No copyright line or footer element should appear on any screen.

In App.tsx and RegistrationScreen.tsx, fix the post-registration navigation flow so that immediately after a successful createUser call, the app transitions directly to the chat list (main screen) without any intermediate steps, loading screens, or delays. The currentUser/profile state must be updated synchronously or before the navigation check runs, so the app never loops back to the registration screen or gets stuck on a loading state.

Permanently remove the Xeta AI feature from the app: delete the XetaAIChatScreen.tsx component, remove the pinned Xeta AI entry from ChatListScreen.tsx (including any FAB or list entry that opens the AI chat), and remove all references to Xeta AI navigation from App.tsx.

Enforce unique usernames on the frontend: in RegistrationScreen.tsx, after a createUser call returns a username-taken error, display a clear, user-readable error message (e.g., 'Username is already taken. Please choose a different one.') inline on the registration form without navigating away.

Make the top header bar and the bottom navigation bar sticky/fixed exclusively on the Chats tab screen (ChatListScreen). Apply position: sticky or fixed CSS to the header and BottomNavigation only when the active tab is 'chats'. On all other tabs (Updates, Calls, Settings) and on inner screens (individual chat, profile, group creation, contacts), the header and bottom nav must scroll normally or remain in their current non-sticky state.
Remove any remaining footer copyright section, copyright text, or footer element from every screen in the app. No copyright line or footer element should appear anywhere in the UI.

Add a voice calling feature accessible from the individual chat screen (ChatScreen.tsx). A phone/voice call icon button must appear in the chat header. Tapping it initiates a voice call to the other user using the browser's WebRTC API (getUserMedia with audio only). Display an in-call overlay UI showing the contact's name/avatar, call duration timer, a mute button, and an end-call button. The CallsScreen (Calls tab) must list recent voice call history entries (caller, recipient, timestamp, call type: voice).

Add a video calling feature accessible from the individual chat screen (ChatScreen.tsx). A video camera icon button must appear in the chat header alongside the voice call button. Tapping it initiates a video call to the other user using WebRTC (getUserMedia with audio and video). Display an in-call overlay with a local video preview (picture-in-picture style), a remote video placeholder, a mute button, a camera toggle button, and an end-call button. The CallsScreen (Calls tab) must also list recent video call history entries (caller, recipient, timestamp, call type: video).

Add a dark/light mode toggle in the Settings tab (ProfileScreen or a dedicated SettingsScreen). The toggle must switch the entire app between the existing dark theme and a light theme. The selected theme preference must persist across sessions (e.g., stored in localStorage). The light theme should use light backgrounds, dark text, and appropriately adjusted accent colors consistent with the Xeta brand palette. Apply the active theme class (e.g., 'dark' or 'light') to the document root so Tailwind dark-mode utilities take effect globally.

In ChatScreen.tsx, make the top contact/user bar (chat header) and the bottom message input bar sticky/fixed so they remain always visible while the user scrolls through the message list. The message list area between the header and input bar must be independently scrollable. This sticky behavior applies only to the individual chat screen and must not affect any other screen.

Fix the 'connection not ready, please try again' error that appears on the RegistrationScreen when the user submits their username and display name. The root cause is that the backend actor (canister connection) is not yet initialized or ready when the registration form is submitted. Ensure the actor is fully initialized before the registration form is interactable or before the createUser call is made. If the actor is still loading, show a loading indicator and disable the submit button rather than allowing the call to fail. If the actor becomes unavailable mid-session, surface a clear, actionable error message (e.g., 'Unable to connect. Please wait a moment and try again.') and allow the user to retry without leaving the registration screen.

In useQueries.ts, audit the useActor hook usage within the registration mutation. Ensure the actor reference used in the createUser call is always the latest authenticated actor instance and is not undefined or uninitialized at the time of the call. Add a guard that throws a user-friendly error (e.g., 'Connection not ready. Please try again.') only if the actor is genuinely unavailable, and re-check actor readiness before every mutation call that touches the backend.

new_feature_requests.summary: User registration and login (phone number or email based authentication)
Real-time one-on-one chat messaging between users
Contacts list showing all registered users or saved contacts
Group chat creation and messaging
Message delivery and read receipts (sent, delivered, read indicators)
Media sharing — send and receive images, videos, and files in chat
User profile with avatar, display name, and status message
Online/last seen presence indicators for contacts

Rebuild the backend in backend/main.mo to support a WhatsApp-style messaging system. Replace the current Bluetooth/conversation model with: user accounts (username, display name, avatar URL, status message, last seen timestamp), contacts (per-user contact lists), one-on-one conversations keyed by a pair of user IDs, group chats (name, member list, admin), messages (sender, content, timestamp, delivery status: sent/delivered/read), and media attachments (image/file URL references). Expose stable Motoko upgrade-safe data structures using HashMap with stable var backup arrays.

Audit and fix the backend createUser function in backend/main.mo to ensure it correctly handles duplicate username and principal checks, returns a clear error variant when registration fails, and persists the new user record reliably so subsequent getMyProfile calls return the newly created profile.
Change the logo position/layout in the app header and increase the logo size to match the 'Xeta' text size

In App.tsx, move the Xeta logo image so it appears to the left of (or inline with) the 'Xeta' app name text in the header, and increase the logo's rendered size so its height visually matches the font size of the 'Xeta' heading text. Both the logo and the text must remain vertically centered and aligned side-by-side.
Add WhatsApp-style bottom navigation bar with four tabs: Chats (chat list), Updates (status/stories), Calls (call history), Settings (app settings & profile), each with matching icons
Add searchable users feature on the chat list screen — search/filter bar to find any registered user by name or username, with all users permanently stored in the backend for persistent accessibility
Increase the logo size in the app top bar to be larger/more prominent
Remove the footer copyright section from the app

Ensure all registered users are permanently and persistently stored in the backend using upgrade-safe stable data structures (stable var backup arrays). Expose a searchUsers or getAllUsers query function on the backend actor so the frontend can retrieve all registered users for search and contact discovery.
After successful account creation/registration, automatically navigate the user directly to the chat list main page without any intermediate steps or delays
Remove Xeta AI feature permanently — delete the pinned Xeta AI chat entry and its entire chat screen/component from the app
Enforce unique usernames — add backend validation in main.mo to reject duplicate usernames during createUser, and surface a clear error on the registration screen if the username is already taken
Make the top header bar and bottom navigation bar sticky/fixed on the main Chats tab screen only; other tabs and inner screens remain unaffected

Enforce unique usernames on the backend: in backend/main.mo, the createUser function must check whether the requested username already exists in the user store before creating a new account. If the username is already taken, return a clear error variant (e.g., #usernameTaken) instead of creating a duplicate entry.
Add voice calling feature — users can make voice calls to each other
Add video calling feature — users can make video calls to each other
Add dark/light mode toggle in the Settings tab so users can switch between dark and bright themes
On the individual chat page, make the top user/contact bar and the bottom message input bar sticky/fixed so they remain visible while scrolling through messages
